name: The 19th Greene
about: The 19th Greene is an indoor golf facility located inside The Greene Turtle restaurant in Deep Creek, Maryland. It utilizes Trackman technology to offer a golfing experience with access to over 200 iconic courses. The venue is designed to be a year-round option, particularly for those looking to play during inclement weather or the off-season. Pricing operates on a per-bay-hour basis, with memberships also available that provide access to the simulators. It is best suited for individuals and groups looking for a casual golf outing combined with dining and social atmosphere.
why_golfers_like_it: Access to 200+ iconic courses via advanced Trackman technology
Indoor play available regardless of weather
Combined golf and dining experience in one venue

food_and_drink.notes: Full food and drink service available through The Greene Turtle restaurant, which is located within the facility.
Full bar service available through The Greene Turtle restaurant.

simulator_hardware.notes: Cutting-edge radar-based launch monitor providing unmatched accuracy for tracking ball and club data.

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The venue does not mention club rentals, so it is recommended to bring your own clubs for the best experience.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: No, outside food and drinks are not permitted. The venue offers a full food and bar service through The Greene Turtle restaurant.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: A typical 18-hole round of golf can take approximately 1-2 hours, depending on the course and number of players.

faq.question: Is this place kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, The 19th Greene is kid-friendly and offers a dedicated bay where children can play Nintendo Switch video games for $35/hr.
